title: Psephellus dealbatus
intro: Psephellus dealbatus, also known as the hoary double daisy or double daisy, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family. Traditionally, Psephellus dealbatus is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Psephellus dealbatus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Psephellus dealbatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant that belongs to the genus Psephellus, which is a group of daisies native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The classification of Psephellus dealbatus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other daisy-like plants such as chrysanthemums and sunflowers. Psephellus dealbatus is often classified as a rare and endangered species due to its limited geographical distribution and habitat.
